mission.title: external-secrets: PushSecrets should implement the same templating options as ExternalSecrets
mission.description: PushSecrets should implement the same templating options as ExternalSecrets. Requested by 14+ users.

steps.title: Check current external-secrets deployment
steps.description:
Verify your external-secrets version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets
helm list -n external-secrets 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working external-secrets installation.

steps.title: Review external-secrets configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant external-secrets configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets
kubectl get configmap -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=external-secrets
```
We want to be able to push non-JSON secrets into HashiCorp vault.

steps.title: Apply the fix for PushSecrets should implement the same templating options as
steps.description:
## Problem Statement

It is not currently possible to use a PushSecret to push a whole Secret resource to a secret store. Since the `secretKey` field is mandatory, only a subset of the secret can be pushed.
Related to #2166
This MR introduce the possibility to not provide a `secretKey`, in this
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: source-secret
  namespace: default
stringData:
  source-key: "this is a secret"
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets
kubectl get events -n external-secrets --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "PushSecrets should implement the same templating options as" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## Problem Statement

It is not currently possible to use a PushSecret to push a whole Secret resource to a secret store. Since the `secretKey` field is mandatory, only a subset of the secret can be pushed.
Related to #2166
This MR introduce the possibility to not provide a `secretKey`, in this case the Secret resource's data field is converted to a JSON string and pushed to the secret store.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: source-secret
  namespace: default
stringData:
  source-key: "this is a secret"

apiVersion: external-secrets.io/v1alpha1
kind: PushSecret
metadata:
  name: pushsecret-example
  namespace: default
spec:
  refreshInterval: 10s
  secretStoreRefs:
    - name: vault-secretstore
      kind: SecretStore
  selector:
    secret:
      name: source-secret # Source Kubernetes secret to be pushed
  data:
    - secretKey: key1
    - secretKey: key2
  remoteRef: vault/secret
  template:
    engineVersion: v2
    data:
      token: {{ key1.token }}
      color: {{ key2.color | replace "red" "blue" }}

apiVersion: external-secrets.io/v1alpha1
kind: PushSecret
metadata:
  name: pushsecret-example
spec:
  # ...
  template:
    # how can we support nested data structures?
    # how do we handle serialization?
    data:
      token: {{ key1.token }}
      color: {{ key2.color | replace "red" "blue" }}

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with external-secrets installed or the issue environment reproducible.
